FEE PAID ON EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR RELIEF FOR LATE FILING (ONE DAY) OF OPPOS TO DEFT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT FILED BY PLAINTIFF BUCCHERI, DANA

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS LEGALMATCH.COM AND MATT GRIFFITH'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AGAINST PLAINTIFF; OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION, SEX DISCRIMINATION. FAVORING A PARAMOUR IS NOT SEX DISCRIMINATION, 807 F. 2D 304; 41 CA 4TH 1626, AND PLAINTIFF DID NOT PRESENT EVIDENCE THAT THE DISCHARGE WAS BECAUSE OF HER SEX. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION, SEXUAL HARRASSMENT. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO WHETHER THERE WAS A HOSTILE WORK ENVIRONMENT. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO THE 3RD AND 4TH CAUSES OF ACTION, RETALIATION AND WRONGFUL DISCHARGE. THE EVIDENCE DISCLOSES THAT 1) THE DISCHARGE OCCURRED SHORTLY AFTER PLAINTIFF COMPLAINED ABOUT THE RELATIONSHIP; 2) GRIFFITH WAS HOSTILE AGAINST PLAINTIFF AFTER FINDING OUT ABOUT THE COMPLAINTS; 3) GRIFFITH HAD A ROLE IN PLAINTIFF'S TERMINATION. PLAINTIFF TO PREPARE PROPOSED ORDER. JUDGE: JOHN K. STEWART, REPORTER: SUSAN CROZIER, CSR #4761

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-10-2008 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Nov-26-2007 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jan-15-08 At 10:00 A.M. In Dept 212

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-07-2007 Text Ruling

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-23-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS LEGALMATCH.COM and MATT GRIFFITH'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AGAINST PLAINTIFF; OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.
APPEARANCES: Melanie D. Popper, Esq. & Verita Gulati of The Revelation Law Firm 510/665-4195 for pltf & opposing party (submitting); no other appearances.
RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Motion for Summary Adjudication is granted as to the 1st cause of action, sex discrimination. Favoring a paramour is not sex discrimination, 807 F. 2d 304; 41 CA 4th 1626, and plaintiff did not present evidence that the discharge was because of her sex. Motion for Summary Adjudication is denied as to the 2nd cause of action, sexual harrassment. There is a triable issue of fact as to whether there was a hostile work environment. Motion for Summary Adjudication is denied as to the 3rd and 4th causes of action, retaliation and wrongful discharge. The evidence discloses that 1) the discharge occurred shortly after plaintiff complained about the relationship; 2) Griffith was hostile against plaintiff after finding out about the complaints; 3) Griffith had a role in plaintiff's termination. Plaintiff to prepare proposed order. (302/JKS/PB) JUDGE: JOHN K. STEWART, REPORTER: SUSAN CROZIER, CSR #4761

MISCELLANEOUS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-13-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Order to Show Cause to defendant's counsel for failure to comply with San Francisco Superior Court Early Settlement Program Policies and Procedures No.4 (Administrative fee) is ordered off calendar/fee paid. Proceedings reported by: Joyce Long CSR# 8620. (212)
